Piet Mondrian

2 works in the gallery.
Click any image to enlarge. Use ← → or close with Esc.
Composition with Red, Blue and Yellow
by Piet Mondrian
De Stijl · 1930
Black lines, three primary colors, white negative space, canvas flat as the grid itself. Mondrian believed he had found the underlying logic of visible reality.
Broadway Boogie Woogie
by Piet Mondrian
Piet Mondrian · 1943 · Museum of Modern Art
Mondrian's last completed painting, made after he fled wartime Europe for New York. The jittering yellow grid mimics Manhattan's street plan and the jazz he had fallen in love with; black bars are banished for the first time in decades.
←Jean-François Millet Claude Monet→
